keyPoints: Provides a dedicated Block Storage platform engineered to next-generation block storage designed to scale IOPS and throughput dynamically, independent of compute engine size..
Integrates Hyperdisk capabilities directly into multi-tier cloud architectures, serving as a key component of the Storage, Backup, and Disaster Recovery Catalog portfolio.
Encourages standard interface structures (REST APIs, Client SDKs, or console controls) to facilitate rapid cloud resource provisioning.
Stores unstructured data as objects in distinct storage classes matched to access frequency schedules.
Transitions data automatically to cheaper archive tiers or deletes expired objects using lifecycle management rules.
Restricts access uniformly using IAM policies at the container level rather than individual file access control lists.
Optimizes Hyperdisk performance through automated platform scaling and resource balancing.
Enables resource segregation, logical naming conventions, and tag-based configuration policies for Hyperdisk.
Integrates with VPC routing and private service access architectures to secure communication tunnels.
Governs access to Hyperdisk using standard IAM roles (e.g. roles/hyperdisk.admin and roles/hyperdisk.viewer) for fine-grained permissions.
Supports audit logging to record administrative actions and verify API configuration modifications for compliance reports.
Secures operational payloads using encryption at rest (Google-managed or Customer-Managed Keys via KMS) and SSL/TLS in transit.
Offers regional and multi-regional configuration options, duplicating service state across multiple zones to sustain outage events.
Enforces API quotas and rate limits programmatically to prevent resource starvation and noisy-neighbor interference.
Provides service recovery tools, regular status logs, and high-availability SLAs matching enterprise uptime goals.
Billed based on resource metrics (e.g., operations count, active connections, or gigabytes stored) allowing cost optimization.
Integrates with Cloud Monitoring and Cloud Logging to enable real-time alerting, metrics tracking, and diagnostic dashboards.
Enables command line administration through the gcloud CLI tool, allowing pipeline automation and scripting.

scenarios.scenario: An enterprise wants to construct a secure architectural pattern involving Hyperdisk that blocks public internet ingress and enforces strict access restrictions.
scenarios.solution: Deploy the Hyperdisk resource inside a VPC Service Control perimeter, routing connections privately using Serverless VPC connectors or internal gateways, and assign permissions using IAM roles.

scenarios.scenario: A development team needs to build automated CI/CD configurations that configure and audit Hyperdisk operations on a regular basis while tracking costs.
scenarios.solution: Use Cloud Build scripts executing 'gcloud' commands to provision the Hyperdisk resources, enable Cloud Audit Logs, and configure budget notifications at the project folder level.

commands.description: Create or configure a new Hyperdisk resource
commands.command:
gcloud hyperdisk create prod-hyperdisk-resource \
  --region=us-central1 \
  --description="Production Hyperdisk Resource"

commands.description: List active Hyperdisk instances and query configuration states
